title: ScheduledWakeups
description: Per-thread durable wakeup entries. Array; multiple wakeups may coexist for the same thread.

wake_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp; gate counts only future-dated entries.

prompt.description: Text injected as first user message on resume.

created_by.description: Tool or process that registered this entry (e.g., 'ScheduleWakeup', 'adapter-fallback').

crash_count.description: Incremented if a wakeup fires while a prior re-spawn for this thread is still active. Crash budget enforcement is v0.1+.

kind.description: Dispatch kind. 'resume' (default if absent, for back-compat) re-injects prompt as a same-session resume. 'check' polls command cheaply; exit 0 means the awaited condition is met and the waker should hand off to a fresh cheap session, while non-zero reschedules without spawning Claude.

command.description: For kind='check': shell command to poll. Exit 0 means condition met and produces a fresh-session handoff; non-zero means not ready and is rescheduled.

escalate_prompt.description: For kind='check': self-contained handoff prompt used when the check succeeds or reaches its deadline. Falls back to prompt if absent.

poll_interval.description: For kind='check': seconds before the next cheap poll when command exits non-zero. Invalid runtime values fall back to the default.

deadline.description: For kind='check': optional time after which an unfinished poll escalates once via handoff instead of polling forever.

model.description: For kind='check': optional model for the fresh handoff session. Defaults to the harness cheap model.
